expectations.evidence: src/lib/turbodocx.ts exists as a TurboSign config/client file

src/lib/turbodocx.ts calls TurboSign.configure({ apiKey: process.env.TURBODOCX_API_KEY!, orgId: process.env.TURBODOCX_ORG_ID!, senderEmail: process.env.TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL!, senderName: process.env.TURBODOCX_SENDER_NAME })
turbosign.controller.ts uses @Controller('signatures'), @Post('send'), @Get(':id/status'); turbosign.service.ts uses @Injectable(); turbosign.module.ts uses @Module()
src/turbosign/turbosign.controller.ts exists as a NestJS controller with @Controller decorator
.env only contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL, TURBODOCX_SENDER_NAME — no TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY found anywhere
No file contains 'Router()' or 'express.Router'. All routing uses NestJS @Controller and @Module patterns
All generated files use .ts extension (turbosign.controller.ts, turbosign.service.ts, turbosign.module.ts, turbodocx.ts, etc.)
